title: The Supreme Truth | Bhagavad Gita 13.13 - 13.17 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Today we are going to discuss from the 13th chapter and the title for the discussion today
is the Supreme Truth based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada.
This is text 13 to 17 what we are going to cover today.
I shall now explain the knowable, knowing which you will taste the eternal.
This is beginningless and it is subordinate to me.
It is called Brahman, the spirit.
And it lies beyond the cause and effect of this material world.
Today these 3-4 verses are quite technical in nature.
explain to them so that we can easily understand.
If you remember, in our previous class we talked about Kshetra and Kshetra Gna.
Like for a farmer, the field of his activity, the land in which he tills the land, cultivates, grows the crops, etc.
Kshetra means field. This body is a Kshetra. Like for a farmer, the field of his activity, the land in which he tills the land, cultivates, grows the crops, etc.
Kshetra means field. This body is a Kshetra. Like for a farmer, the field of his activity, the land in which he tills the land, cultivates, grows the crops, etc.
Kshetra means field. This body is a Kshetra. Like for a farmer, the field of his activity, the land in which he tills the land, cultivates, grows the crops, etc.
Kshetra means field. This body is a Kshetra. Like for a farmer, the field of his activity, the land in which he tills the land, cultivates, grows the crops, etc.
Kshetra means field. This body is a Kshetra. Like for a farmer, the field of his activity, the land in which he tills the land, cultivates, grows the crops, etc.
Kshetrajna. Kshetrajna is the soul. In this Kshetra, there are two Kshetrajnas. What are
the two Kshetrajnas? The soul and the super soul. Now some of you were there, some of
you were not there. The Kshetra comprises of 24 elements. Let's do a quick recap what
those 24 elements are. So five gross elements, earth, air, water, fire, ether. Five senses,
Karmendriyas and five Gyanendriyas. So we have five working senses and five knowledge
acquiring senses. What we generally talk about senses is Gyanendriyas, eyes, ears,
nose, tongue and touch skin. Through these senses we acquire knowledge, it's called Gyanendriyas.
So we have five material elements. We have five Gyanendriyas and then we have five Karmendriyas,
five working senses. Our hands, legs, genitals and mouth. And then we have five sense objects.
Slip Swi- vector rule. And then we have five sense objects like for the eyes, ears and
tongue. So we have those bones, tongue, and touch are these parts of which Gyanendriyas.
so we have those bones, tongue, aired areas where our senses are meant to go and we will
know things through the senses. So when we bring the sense ofako Männer & sense ofoka Menada,
we are ready for the middle and lower body and inner body. When we take notes, sense objects,
when doing these centersk, when doing these satisfied senses. So it's painful to listen and
For eyes, it is Rupam. For the tongue, it is taste. For ears, it is Shabda. For skin, it is Sparsha.
And what is left now? For the nose, it is Gandha, smell. So it becomes twenty.
Five sense objects, five working senses, five knowledge acquiring senses and five gross elements. Twenty.
Then we have three subtle elements, Manas, Buddhi and Ahankara. That makes it twenty-three.
And the twenty-fourth element is consciousness under the influence of the modes.
Under the influence of contaminated consciousness, Rajoguna, Tamoguna, Satoguna is the twenty-fourth element.
So these twenty-four elements and then there is soul and super-soul.
And all what we see in this world is interaction of these twenty-four elements, the soul, super-soul, these twenty-four elements under the influence of Kala.
So there is Kshetra and there is Kshetragna.
Now, the Lord is going to explain about the Kshetragna.
Who that Kshetragna is, who that supreme knower is, the super-soul is.
And then he says, if we have a proper understanding of Kshetra and Kshetragna,
We can relish nectar of life.
Sometimes people get a feeling that spirituality is all about abnegating, rejecting happiness.
It's about voluntarily accepting some pains.
As if it is a sin to enjoy life.
So lot of people are, you know, we want to enjoy life.
And it appears that those who are practicing spiritual life are against that principle.
They don't want to enjoy life.
They want to probably suffer, suffer and suffer so that they get mukti.
So here it is said, they can enjoy nectar in life.
Broadly speaking, there are two kinds of happiness.
We have discussed this many times in the session.
What is the broad distinction, difference between material happiness and spiritual happiness?
Spiritual happiness brings happiness at the same time, brings a good quality.
But spiritual happiness is all the time happy.
So you are saying spiritual happiness is all the time happy.
Including at the time of chanting.
But we feel sleepy and we feel tired and we feel bored.
But spiritual happiness is all the time happy.
So you are saying material happiness is not as out of picture.
It is all to do with interaction of the kshetra, the senses, sense objects.
Yeah? And spiritual happiness is to do with the interaction between the soul and the super-soul.
Okay? Anybody else? Broad difference between... Yes, Deepak.
It is hidden. You have to find out. You have to labor hard. Okay, anybody else?
We just discussed this. It is right. It is temporary and it is eternal.
One very very important distinction for a material happiness and spiritual happiness is,
material happiness is nectar in the beginning, poison in the end.
Spiritual happiness is poison in the beginning, apparently.
Some of the devotees had gone. They said in the morning if you touch water it looks like electric shock.
So cold. So it is apparently poison in the beginning.
Lot of rules and regulations. You like enjoying but you are said don't eat it.
You are advised to fast where you have a natural inclination or desire to eat anything and everything you want.
So it is apparently lot of restrictions, rules, do's and don'ts.
Apparently poison in the beginning, nectar in the end.
And only in the human form of life, this is very important for us to appreciate, understand.
Only in human form of life we have got the developed intelligence to discern and to keep in abeyance,
immediate happiness for long term higher pleasure.
And this principle we apply even in our day to day life, even if when we are not a spiritualist.
I know many people, they are working in good MNCs but they stop working, they take a bank loan, 20 lakh rupees, get admitted into an MBA college.
They have stopped earning, they have taken a break from their career, they have invested 20 lakh rupees,
taken the pain of going to a college, studying 8-10 hours a day.
Immediately it appears that they are going through lot of hardships. Yes or no?
But long term it is good for them, beneficial for them.
So even in our material life we see many times we, for a long term good, we are willing to work.
For a long term good, we are willing to take some short term austerities.
And in human form of life specifically, to enjoy this nectar, the real nectar, which is spiritual happiness,
tapo divyam shudhyet brahma saukhyam tu anantam.
It is said that human life is meant for tapo divyam.
What kind of tapasya? Divyam. Divine.
Not one's own mind, one concocts some kind of a tapasya.
For example chanting. It is a tapasya.
At times we may not like doing it. We may not feel good doing it.
At times it may look very boring.
Tapo divyam, when we do that tapasya, what will happen? Shudhyet, we will get purified.
All that Kshetra which has become contaminated, the senses are helplessly getting pulled towards sense objects.
And the Kshetragna is struggling in that Kshetra.
Tapo divyam shudhyet, what will happen? Shudhyet, we will get purified.
And with that purified consciousness, what will we experience?
We will experience happiness which is untainted, which is lasting.
We are eligible for, we are qualified for, we have entitlement for enjoying that kind of happiness.
But unfortunately, we have settled for something which is very very meager.
And that material happiness, our Shastra says,
it is available, if you analyze, what is that topmost material happiness
which people at times even get into scandals.
A lot of people, it's material happiness there.
But that happiness is abundantly available even in other life forms.
A dog is enjoying that kind of a happiness.
The senses coming in contact with the sense objects.
So, here the Lord is saying, one can relish nectar in this life with proper understanding of Shetra,
Shetragna and the Supreme Lord, the Supersoul, the Supreme Shetragna.
Now what about this Shetragna, the Supreme Knower, the Supersoul?
Everywhere are His hands and legs.
In this way, the Supersoul exists.
So the Supersoul, the Shetragna, the Soul is knower of only this field, this body.
Whereas the Supersoul is knower of all the bodies.
Now how is it possible that a person knows everything?
Just like a sun may be situated in one place in the sky but it diffuses sunlight everywhere.
You might fly to Delhi and then again when you look up on the sky you see that sun is there.
The same sun in the sky is diffusing heat and light everywhere.
Likewise the same Supreme Lord is present everywhere, in everyone, in every atom as Supersoul.
Everywhere are His hands and legs, His eyes and faces and He hears everything.
Material life consists of the understanding that eat, drink, be merry, enjoy life.
We are not accountable for our actions.
So long as I can beat the law, I can go scot free, I should be happy.
But a devotee understands that I might cheat the law.
I might even apparently go scot free.
I might be intelligent enough to break the laws and not get caught.
But not with the case of the Supreme Lord.
So for a devotee, for a materialist, he is conscious that I should not get caught.
For a devotee, he is more conscious of displeasing the Lord.
He has that conscience which always keeps him on the right track.
For a materialist, it will be very displeasing to the Lord.
He is conscious of the pleasure and displeasure of the Lord.
One time there was a Gurukul and typical Gurukul where the disciples go and get trained under the guidance of a Guru.
So their training got completed, three of them.
And generally we have, even in academics, after the session gets over, the academic session, we have an exam.
So the Guru, just to test whether they have learned their lessons sufficiently well, he gave them a final test.
And the test was, he gave each one of them an apple in their hand and they said,
Go and eat this apple and come back.
But only one condition you have to keep in mind, that do not eat the apple in the presence of anyone.
Eat the apple in a place where no one is watching you.
So the disciples, for them, they said, what a simple test.
After two, three years of all the training, such a simple test the Guru has given.
So one of them went the backyard of the ashram, Gurukul, there was a forest.
And typically in forest you don't find human beings.
He went deep in the forest, ensured that nobody is watching, ate the apple and came back.
So he was, you know, big smile on his face, he has, you know, final exam he has cleared, he has passed.
The second one, who was relatively more wise, more intelligent, he said,
Maybe, it's not the human beings which are not watching, but there are many birds, many insects, they also have eyes.
They also are living entities, they are watching.
So he went deep in the forest, looked for a cave and when he entered the cave, it was pitch dark.
And after entering the cave with a boulder, he made sure that the entry point of the cave is also closed and it was pitch dark.
Even if there are some insects, there is nobody going to watch in that dark, this thing.
He ate the apple and came back.
Heart of heart, he had this poor fellow, the first one who had come, you know, he has failed the test.
I have passed the test because I have eaten the apple when nobody was watching.
And late in the evening, the third disciple, he comes back with a sullen face, very disappointed, apple still in his hand.
And he was quite dejected that, you know, such a simple test, unfortunately I have failed that test.
I couldn't find a place where I could eat the apple and nobody was watching.
So then one of them asked, didn't you find a place?
He said, wherever I went, I found, I couldn't escape two eyes watching me.
So then one of them said, you could have come with me.
I went deep inside a cave, it was pitch dark, you could have told me you are not finding the place, I would have taken you there.
He said, I even went in that pitch dark cave, but still I could not escape those two eyes.
So I have come back convinced that there is no place in the world where I can escape the eyes of the Lord.
The Guru got up and embraced the disciple.
You have actually understood the message, you are the realized one.
So what brings accountability in the life of a devotee?
Why he becomes a good devotee, a sincere devotee?
Becomes a thorough gentleman because he knows that the Lord is always watching him.
Moment he does wrong, it signals him that yes, he had made a mistake.
When he by mistake kills an ant, it feels bad.
Now, the Lord is present everywhere.
That's why in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says,
If you offer me with love, a flower, a fruit, a water, I will accept it.
We have examples of great devotees who have even done Manas Puja.
Manas Puja means even worshipping the Lord in the mind.
Because they could not afford to do Puja with gross objects.
That's why they said Bhavagrahi Janardana.
He is more interested in our Bhava, in our love, in our devotion.
And if we have that, He will accept it.
The Supersoul is the original source of all senses.
Although He is the maintainer of all living beings,
He transcends the modes of nature.
And at the same time, He is the master of all modes of material nature.
So, here it is said that the Supersoul is the original source of all senses.
So, there are philosophers who say that,
This is the word which says that Lord is impersonal.
He does not have a form.
In other words, He is impersonal.
The Supersoul is the original source of all senses.
And hence, the Lord is also called Nirguna.
We have material senses made of gross elements.
The Lord's senses are all spiritual.
They are untouched by the modes.
And hence, the Lord is also called Nirguna.
This is not to say that,
He does not have a form.
I can see only what is within my...
This question I have asked earlier.
Some of you are not there.
Next week in Gita Live class,
Will you come or not come?
You don't have a free will.
Something interesting comes up in your life.
So, is the future hard coded?
How many of us feel it is hard coded?
That is what the church in the 7th century AD believed that,
The Lord makes a person fatalistic.
Destiny is the one which overrules everything.
And at times it made a person lazy.
If it is written in my destiny to pass,
If God wants me to come to Gita Life,
If God wants me to watch TV,
So, does the Lord know the future?
The answer is both Yes and No.
When a cow is tied to a pole with a rope,
If the rope which is tying the cow to a pole is 5 meters long,
The cow has freedom of moving in a 5 meter radius freely.
But beyond 5 meter, the rope restricts that cow.
And all of us have an invisible rope tying us.
If the rope length is 0 meters,
The example is given like a tree.
It's just standing in one place.
It's a living entity like you and me.
And so here we have example of a living entity where length of the rope is practically 0.
So we also are bound and free to different degrees.
Some people are so bound, so bound, the Lord can very clearly say,
He cannot come because He is so bad.
And some of us, just like the cow,
Has a freedom within 5 meter radius.
We can exercise our free will.
And choose to come or not come.
So the answer is both yes and no.
Based on the choices we make every moment,
Everything is not written in destiny.
So I am just a puppet.
It's all written in my fate.
Every moment we have a choice.
If we make the right choices,
The length of the rope increases.
The length of the rope decreases.
If we continuously make wrong, wrong, wrong choices,
The length of rope becomes practically 0.
So the more the length of the rope,
The Lord has given that exercise of free will to the living entity,
Just like even in the father,
Just like a father gives some pocket money to a child,
The father will give more money.
If he knows that the son has taken that money,
And got into a bad habit,
Who was the President of the United States,
What's the secret of your success?
What's the secret of your success?
You become President of the United States,
Almost like the number one man in the world.
As if everything is dependent on,
As if everything is dependent on,
We go to the other extreme.
We depend on Lord in our activities,
That's why there's a verse which says,
The cause of the result of your activities,
And do not be a devout,
And do not be attached to,
What motivates us to do our actions,
Then what is the motivation to act?
We have to do our duty,
Not a blade of grass moves,
Not a blade of grass moves,
Not a blade of grass moves,
But I will have to do my,
What's going to be the weather,
What's going to be our future.
using our own material logic, using our own limited intelligence, we can come to a wrong conclusion.
That's why it is important that we get the right understanding through our Shastras,
through great Acharyas coming in disciplic succession.
If the Lord is all present, we have in our experience that a person is limited by time and space.
So if the Lord is all present means he is not a person. It's a material logic.
There is so much of misery in this world and Lord is supreme controller.
Not a blade of grass moves without his sanction. Because so many things are going wrong here, therefore Lord is not there.
You are there because I am able to see you. I am not able to see the Lord, that means Lord is not there.
So with this material logic, material rational, we will definitely come to a wrong understanding.
And many times people also, like I said fatalistic theory, everything is not a blade of grass moves without Lord's sanction.
So let me relax. If it has to happen, it will happen.
One time there was a priest. He prayed, prayed and prayed, O Lord, please protect me.
And there was a weather forecast. According to forecast, it's going to rain very heavily.
And there was a possibility the entire village in which the church was there will get inundated with water. Floods will come.
But the priest said, no Lord, please protect me, please save me.
He ignored the weather forecast and he refused to go away from that village.
There was a forecast and people started evacuating that village.
So he did not leave. He just kept on praying.
And then actually it started raining and the whole village started flooding with water.
Even the water entered the church and this priest was praying, O Lord, please protect me.
Finally, when the water had almost, you know, inundated the church, the priest was holding on and praying.
Someone came in a boat also and said, now the village is getting flooded.
Please, if you want, come inside the village. Let's escape from this place.
He said, no, the Lord will protect. The Lord will protect and continued praying.
So the boatman had to return back disappointed.
I have told him, I even offered help. I even offered him, he can come in the boat. He refused.
And somehow it so happened the water kept on rising, kept on rising and the priest had no option but to go to the top of the church and cling on to the cross which was there on the top of the church.
And said, Lord, please protect. Lord, please protect.
And finally he saw in the sky there was a helicopter.
And that helicopter threw a staircase and says, please hold on to the case and come up.
He said, no, the Lord will protect. The Lord will protect. Lord is our saviour.
And even that was not heard.
Finally, unfortunately, the whole village got flooded including the church got submerged and the priest also died.
So because he was quite pious, he decided to go to the top of the church.
And because he was quite pious, he went to the heaven and met the Lord.
He said, Lord, what is this? I prayed so long, so many, so fervently.
Please save me. Please save me. But you know, you didn't answer my prayers. I had to finally die.
So he said, I sent you a forecast. You did not listen.
I sent you a boat. You did not listen.
I sent you even a helicopter to save you but you did not listen. What can I do?
So in other words, the Lord is very active in our lives.
Unfortunately, when we are materially overwhelmed, we are not able to hear the Lord.
We are not able to experience the reciprocation of the Lord.
It is said that the Lord is Shaktimaan and the Lord has His Shaktis.
We are interested more in the Shaktis than the Shaktimaan.
When Duryodhan was asked, what do you want? You want my army or you want me?
Duryodhan was very happy. I want Shakti. I want the army.
What will I do with the Shaktimaan?
Because Shaktimaan is never without his Shakti.
A Shaktimaan is always with his Shakti.
So a devotee is more interested in the Shaktimaan than in the Shakti.
Some people are interested in worshipping only Lakshmi but may not be interested in Narayana.
Ravana was interested in Sita, was not interested in Ram.
A question to all of you. Should we do Lakshmi Puja, yes or no?
How many of you say we should do Lakshmi Puja?
Is Lakshmi Puja right or wrong?
It is a very blunt question I am asking because we know in India we have an age-old tradition.
We do Lakshmi Puja, we do Saraswati Puja. Is it right or wrong?
How many of you say it is right?
How many of you say it is not required, just worship Shaktimaan and that is enough?
There is nothing wrong if we worship Lakshmi with the right understanding.
The right understanding is Lakshmi is consort of Narayana.
Bless me with prosperity so that I can serve Narayana.
The wrong understanding is bless me Lakshmi so that I can enjoy you.
If you want blessings of Lakshmi and you want to enjoy Lakshmi, keeping Lord in the side, the mentality is that of a Ravana.
We will be ruined in the long term.
Nothing wrong in worshipping Lakshmi but with the understanding Lakshmi is consort of Narayana.
Let me be blessed with Lakshmi so that I can serve Lord Narayana.
Both Ravana also wanted Sita. Hanuman also wanted Sita.
Hanuman went all the way to Lanka to get Sita.
But one wanted Sita to enjoy.
And we call him Ravana, demon.
And we worship Hanuman because he also wanted Sita for Lord Ramachandra.
So if we worship Lakshmi with the mentality, let me be blessed with prosperity.
And I am nowhere having Narayana in my consciousness.
Nothing wrong in being blessed by Lakshmi.
But what is wrong is we forget Narayana when we are blessed with Lakshmi.
Devotee who has developed love of the Supreme Lord,
He can experience the reciprocation of the Lord.
For him, Lord is not just a matter of blind belief or some kind of a faith.
Although the Supersoul appears to be divided, he is never divided.
Although he is the maintainer of every living entity, it is to be understood that he devours and develops.
Now the Lord is situated in everyone's heart.
So does that mean that he is divided?
One small portion of the Lord is there in your heart, one portion of the Lord in my heart, one portion of the Lord in his heart.
He is present in everyone, in everything, yet he is undivided.
Again crude example, the sun is in the sky.
If you go to Delhi, you see sun is still in the sky.
You go to Los Angeles, sun is still in the sky.
The same sun appears in Los Angeles, same sun appears in Delhi, same sun appears in Mumbai.
Same Supreme Lord is present in my heart, in your heart, in everyone's heart.
Supreme Lord although is the maintainer of every living entity,
the maintainer of every living entity devours everything at the time of annihilation.
The Kala is working under his direction.
Mrityu sarva haras chaham, I am the all devouring death.
Kala, time factor which devours everything in due course of time is an agent of Supreme Lord.
Can we have one more story?
How many of us want a story?
So one time Akbar was in the Darbar.
All the ministers, the typical assembly was on.
And one person comes in and gives Akbar a very intricately carved ivory fan.
So gift to Akbar and Akbar took that fan and you know he used the fan for a while.
And Birbal was sitting next to him.
He threw the fan at him and said Birbal this fan is for you.
Give this fan to someone who is a greater fool than you.
In the whole assembly in front of everyone indirectly he told Birbal you are a fool.
He said give this fan to someone who is a greater fool than you.
So Birbal as we all know was quite intelligent.
He waited for the right time.
And a time came when Akbar became quite old.
So Birbal went to see Akbar.
And then he saw he was quite despondent, quite sad.
So he said your majesty looks like you are not feeling well.
You look quite sullen, quite disappointed.
He said ya now that inevitable journey I have to go for that inevitable journey.
The soon the agents of death.
And I will have to go with them.
Which journey you are talking about?
You fool that inevitable journey which all of us have to eventually embark on.
Soon the angels of death will come and we will have to leave.
I am sure you would have prepared for that journey.
He said that is the sad part.
Unfortunately I have not prepared for it.
And very soon I have to embark on that journey.
So Birbal took that fan from his hand and said.
This fan actually should be with you.
You are knowing that you have to embark on this inevitable journey.
Still you did not prepare for it.
Who could be a greater fool than you?
So human form of life affords us a wonderful opportunity.
With the right understanding of where we are heading.
What are the laws which are operating in this world.
One who has taken birth as a human being.
And dies like a cat and a dog.
What is the meaning of miser?
Miser means he has lot of wealth.
But he is reluctant to spend that money.
A person who has got this human body.
He has a great ability to understand these things.
But if he dies in ignorance.
We can die conscious of Lord.
But if we die like cat and dog.
We have wasted this human form of life.
This world has come into being because of him.
